title: Prevention of sensors' damage in optical systems
abstract: An optical arrangement comprising: a light beam generating source for generating an optical signal; an elliptic polarizer (e.g. a circular polarizer); a transmitter; a receiver; and a sensor, and wherein the optical arrangement is characterized in that at least part of the optical signal arrives to the transmitter for its transmission towards a target, after it has been elliptically polarized.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. An optical arrangement which comprises: a light beam generating source for generating an optical signal ; an elliptic polarizer; a transmitter; a receiver; and a sensor, and wherein the optical arrangement is characterized in that at least part of the optical signal arrives to the transmitter for its transmission towards a target, after it has been elliptically polarized. 2. The optical arrangement of claim 1, further comprising a filter for preventing conveyance to the sensor of signals that are counter elliptically polarized, arriving from a target comprising one or more highly reflective surfaces and received by the receiver. 3. The optical arrangement of claim 1, further comprising a splitter configured to split into two parts an optical signal which has been elliptically polarized, wherein the first part is conveyed to said sensor for use as a reference signal thereat, and the second part is conveyed to said transmitter to be transmitted as an optical signal towards the target. 4. The optical arrangement of claim 1, wherein the elliptical polarization is a circular polarization. 5. The optical arrangement of claim 1 adapted to operate in a non-coherent optical detection system, wherein said arrangement 

comprises at least one linear polarizer, and wherein the optical signal generated by the light beam generating source is linearly polari zed by one of the at least one linear polarizer 6. The optical arrangement of claim 5, wherein the elliptic polari zer is configured to elliptically polarize the first part of the linearly polarized optical signal, and to block the signal reflected from the target and which arrives at a counter ellipt ic polarization to the receiver. 7. A method for use in an optical detection system, comprising the steps of : (i) generating an optical signal by a light beam generating source ; (ii) polarizing at least part of the optical signal by an elliptic polarizer; (iii) transmitting at least part of the elliptically polarized optical signal towards a target; (iv) receiving an optical signal reflected from the target following the arrival of the transmitted signal thereat; and (v) blocking optical signals that were reflected and received in a counter elliptic polarization from reaching a sensor of said optical detection system. The method of claim 7, wherein the elliptical polarization r s a circular polarization. 9. The method of claim 7 for use in a non-coherent optical detection system, wherein aid method further comprising the step of linearly polarizing the optical signal generated by the light beam generating source. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising a step of elliptically polarizing the first part of the linearly polarized optical signal, and blocking the signal reflected from the target which arrives at a counter elliptic polarization to the system receiver, by an elliptic polarizer.
